title: 'He said if the kids pulled off the job, there'd be many more': The strange foiled attack on Bank of America in Paris
subhed: Three teenagers and a 21-year-old man were placed in detention as part of the investigation into the attempted bombing of Bank of America's office in Paris. Investigators suspect that Iranian intelligence services were behind the plot.

title: Trump threatens to annihilate Iranian civilian infrastructure, risking major escalation
Train driver killed as high-speed TGV crashes into truck in northern France
Oil giants' profits flow partly into tax havens
subhed: A study by the International Tax Observatory finds that during commodity booms, multinational extractive companies – including oil groups – channel a larger share of their profits into low-tax countries.

title: Israel's rule of law and democracy are on the brink of collapse
subhed: The introduction of the death penalty for Palestinians convicted of 'a terrorist act,' but not for Israeli Jews guilty of abuses and the murder of civilians in the occupied West Bank, is emblematic of the ongoing transformation of Israel.

title: With 'Normes Corps' at the Palais de Tokyo, vulnerable bodies unite their strength
subhed: For its new season, the Paris contemporary art center is presenting six solo exhibitions by artists exploring fragility, disability and difference.

title: Viktor Medvedchuk, the Ukrainian traitor Putin wanted to install in place of Zelensky
subhed: Captured in April 2022 as he tried to flee Ukraine, the former MP and oligarch now lives in exile in Russia. As a lawyer in Kyiv in 1980, he sent his client, the great Ukrainian poet Vasyl Stus, to his death.

Are Trump's ideologues intellectuals or imposters?
subhed: Curtis Yarvin has become a leading figure in the Neo-Reactionary movement (also known as the Dark Enlightenment), which has now made it to the White House. But can this blogger really be considered an intellectual? Political theorist Arnaud Miranda and sociologist Olivier Alexandre debate the origins and roles of one of the thinkers who has Trump's ear.

title: On immigration, Spain moves against the tide of its European neighbors
subhed: Citing economic and moral arguments, Spanish Prime Minister Pedro Sanchez's Socialist government is preparing to grant legal status to 500,000 undocumented workers, most from Latin America. The plan brings hope to the 840,000 foreign nationals living without papers in Spain.

title: Why is Donald Trump so mean?
subhed: By shamelessly flouting democratic norms and flaunting his amorality, brutality and bad faith, the American president restores pride to his humiliated electoral base, unsettles his opponents, and subverts the rules of the political game.
